title: Tunisian Couscous Pilaf
description: A herbal and pillowy Tunisian dish featuring couscous with an al-dente finish, perfect for a lazy Sunday, this low-glycemic-friendly recipe is a must try. It combines the flavors of the Mediterranean with the comfort of a hearty pilaf.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Stir in the dried thyme, dried rosemary, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan, and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the whole wheat couscous to the saucepan, cover, and turn off the heat.
Let the couscous steam for 5 minutes, then fluff it with a fork.
Stir in the lemon juice and serve the pilaf hot.
Garnish with fresh herbs, if desired.
